findings.description: Docstring claims strict parsing but strptime with %Y-%m-%d accepts non-zero-padded values like '2021-1-1', so 'strictly' overstates the guarantee.
int() truncates toward zero, so a moment fractionally in the future (e.g. -0.4s) yields seconds==0 and returns 'just now' instead of 'in the future'.
Weeks bucket has no upper bound, so very old moments render as e.g. '200 w ago' with no months/years granularity (docstring says 'coarse', so arguably intended).
clamp_to_range does not validate tzinfo consistency (unlike humanize_delta), so mixed aware/naive bounds raise an undocumented TypeError on comparison.
business_days_between materializes the full date list via day_range (O(n) memory) where a closed-form weekday count would be O(1).
day_range eagerly builds a full list that can be huge for wide ranges; a generator would bound memory.
start_of_day/day_range accept a datetime (subclass of date) and silently drop its time/tz, violating the implied date-only contract without error.
is_weekend lacks a docstring while every other public function has one, breaking the module's documentation consistency.
